title: Rich Roasted Canadian Tomato Chowder
description: A briny and silky Canadian dish featuring roasted tomatoes and a macrobiotic-friendly twist, perfect for midnight cravings. This chowder is made with fresh tomato juice and a blend of aromatic spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the fresh tomatoes in half and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the olive oil over the tomatoes and sprinkle with sea salt and black pepper.
Roast the tomatoes in the oven for 20 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly caramelized.
Remove the tomatoes from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Peel the skin off the tomatoes and puree them in a blender or food processor until smooth.
In a large pot, combine the roasted tomato puree, canned tomato juice, sliced onion, minced garlic, dried dill weed, and smoked paprika.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat and let cook for 15 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the onion is tender.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and season the chowder with additional sea salt and black pepper if needed.
Serve the chowder hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
